opportunities.title: Your Mac, Your Telegram, Your Wallet: The New Session-Hijack Chain Nobody's Patching For
opportunities.why_now: SlowMist just documented live macOS malware that steals Telegram sessions specifically to drain crypto wallets, and most self-custody guides still don't cover session-token theft.
opportunities.angle: Technical breakdown of the attack chain + a checklist of what 'secure enough' actually means for Telegram-linked wallets

opportunities.title: Ostium's Oracle Just Got Exploited for Millions — Here's the Line Every Perp Protocol Keeps Crossing
opportunities.why_now: A multimillion-dollar oracle exploit just forced Ostium to halt trading, the latest in a long, predictable line of oracle-manipulation hacks.
opportunities.angle: Post-mortem style piece mapping this exploit against past oracle failures to show the pattern is structural, not accidental

opportunities.title: Humanity Protocol Will 'Prioritize OpSec' Now — After Losing $36M, Like Everyone Else Does
opportunities.why_now: The protocol's post-hack pledge to focus on security is a near-perfect example of the industry's reactive-only security culture.
opportunities.angle: Sharp opinion piece using the quote as a jumping-off point: why 'security after the hack' is the default crypto operating model

opportunities.title: Even Airbnb's CEO Got His X Account Hacked to Shill Tokenization — What Chance Do You Have?
opportunities.why_now: A high-profile exec account was compromised within days and used to post AI-generated crypto content, showing social hijacks remain the easiest crypto attack vector.
opportunities.angle: Fast-turn commentary tying exec-account hacks to the broader pattern of social media as crypto's weakest link, with practical account-hardening tips

opportunities.title: SummerFi Shuts Down After Seven Years — Killed by an Exploit, Not the Market
opportunities.why_now: A seven-year-old DeFi protocol just cited a hack as the reason it's winding down entirely, a stark reminder that security debt eventually comes due.
opportunities.angle: Retrospective/eulogy format: what SummerFi got right for years and the one exploit that ended it, framed as a cautionary tale for 'mature' protocols
